description: Provider-agnostic multi-agent project management: skills for Claude and Gemini + PTY overlay shell

tasks.title: Cross-provider atomic task claiming — claimed_by field + claim protocol
tasks.description: Enable simultaneous Claude + Gemini orchestrators on the same tasks.json without task collision. (1) Add claimed_by field to task schema (null = unclaimed, 'claude'/'gemini' = in use). (2) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: before spawning a worker for a task, read tasks.json, check status=pending AND claimed_by=null, write claimed_by='claude' under a file lock (.agentflow/tasks.lock), then spawn. On task complete/failed, clear claimed_by. (3) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same protocol with claimed_by='gemini'. (4) Both providers skip tasks where claimed_by is already set. File lock ensures atomic read-modify-write; lock held <100ms. Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert claim protocol present in both Claude and Gemini skill content.

tasks.title: Cross-provider rate headroom check before task claiming
tasks.description: Before claiming a task, each provider checks its own rate_calibration_<provider>.json for remaining 5hr window headroom. If remaining headroom < 10% of cap_5hr, skip claiming this round and log 'Rate headroom low — skipping claim (<provider>)'. This prevents a nearly-rate-limited provider from grabbing tasks it cannot complete before the window resets, allowing the other provider to continue. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: add headroom check step before claim in the task loop; read rate_calibration_claude.json, compute remaining = cap_5hr * (1 - end_pct_5hr/100), skip if remaining < cap_5hr * 0.10. (2) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same with rate_calibration_gemini.json. Depends on T-063 (claimed_by protocol) and T-060 (provider-keyed files).

tasks.title: Per-task token estimator — regression model from task_token_log.jsonl
tasks.description: Build empirical estimator to replace the 2500-token constant in round-sizing. (1) New file agentflow/shadow/task_estimator.py: reads ~/.agentflow/task_token_log.jsonl. (2) Requires sample_count >= 5; else returns fallback constant 2500. (3) Fits linear regression: tokens ~ intercept + slope_lines * estimated_lines + slope_files * file_count using least squares (stdlib only — no numpy; implement normal equations directly). (4) Writes model to ~/.agentflow/task_estimator.json: {slope_lines, slope_files, intercept, r2, sample_count, updated_at}. (5) Exposes estimate(estimated_lines, file_count) -> int. (6) Tests: test_task_estimator.py — synthetic log entries; assert model written; assert fallback on low sample count; assert estimate() output reasonable.

tasks.title: Orchestrate skill — parallel worker scheduling using token estimator
tasks.description: Replace sequential per-round task execution with parallel scheduling bounded by token budget. (1) commands/claude/orchestrate.md: at round start, load ~/.agentflow/task_estimator.json (fallback 2500 if absent or sample_count < 5). (2) Compute N = max(1, floor(remaining_tokens / estimated_tokens_per_task)). (3) Filter candidate tasks: exclude any task whose owns fields overlap with another selected task (disjoint owns required to prevent write conflicts). (4) Spawn up to N workers in parallel Agent calls within the same orchestrate session. (5) commands/gemini/skills/orchestrate/SKILL.md: same parallel scheduling logic with task_estimator.json load. (6) Tests: test_orchestrate_skill.py — assert parallel scheduling logic, estimator load step, and owns-disjoint check present in both Claude and Gemini skill content.

tasks.title: Model Routing Savings — track USD and token-class savings in combined report
tasks.description: Extend agentflow reporting to calculate and display savings gained by routing subagent tasks to cheaper models (e.g. Claude Haiku vs. Sonnet). (1) Read agentflow_ledger.json sessions and log entries. (2) For each subagent run, check if a cheaper model (Haiku) was selected instead of the premium default (Sonnet). (3) Compute the shadow cost (what it would have cost on Sonnet) vs the actual cost (Haiku). (4) Integrate 'model_routing' as a new savings strategy row and daily trend in report_builder.py and combined_report.html. (5) Tests: write a test verifying the cost differential calculation and HTML output rendering.

tasks.title: Haiku vs Sonnet subagent A/B — measure output token delta from model routing
tasks.description: Run a controlled A/B to measure how many output tokens subagents save when routed to Haiku vs Sonnet. (1) PTY shell coin-flip at session start writes haiku/sonnet to .agentflow/model_ab_arm.txt alongside verbosity_ab_arm.txt. (2) proxy/server.py already extracts `model` from each request — log {ts, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, session_type} to .agentflow/model_ab_log.jsonl per completed request (skip streaming chunks; write on request close). (3) agentflow/shadow/model_ab.py: compute per-arm output token stats (mean, p90, n, 95% CI) and persist to .agentflow/model_ab_baseline.json. (4) report_builder.py: add a model-routing row showing measured output-token delta × n_subagent_turns and annotate with CI. Stopping criterion: n>=30 per arm AND CI width < 100 tokens. Depends on T-101 (arm tagging pattern) and T-102 (stopping criterion signal in report). Do not begin until verbosity A/B stopping signal fires.

tasks.title: Gemini Oracle Skill — create /oracle equivalent skill for Gemini
tasks.description: Implement /oracle skill for Gemini. Create commands/gemini/skills/oracle/SKILL.md (registered as .agents/skills/oracle/SKILL.md) outlining the Gemini Oracle flow. Update test_oracle_skill.py to assert correct structure for both Claude and Gemini.

tasks.title: PTY idle wake-up — inject b"\r" after 60s idle with task in flight
tasks.description: Add idle wake-up to session_manager.py: if _task_in_flight (tasks_in_flight.json exists) AND no PTY output for 60s AND input field is empty (post-output silence: _last_had_content was True, now no output for > 1s) → write_input("/orchestrate\r") to re-enter orchestrate skill. Track idle time via _last_output_ts (float, set to time.monotonic() in _handle_output on every non-empty chunk). Backoff: double interval on each injection with no state change (task still in flight after wake-up response); cap at 300s. Reset backoff when state changes. Guard: only inject when _state_machine.state == States.TASK_RUNNING (task legitimately in flight, not just idle between user turns). Config key: shell.idle_wakeup_seconds (default 60); set to 0 to disable. Tests: test_idle_wakeup_fires_after_threshold, test_idle_wakeup_respects_guard_state, test_idle_wakeup_backoff_doubles. Depends on T-121 (reactor must be stable) and T-125 (pty_signal task_done used to detect in-flight). [CANCELLED: process liveness via waitpid in T-121 is the complete signal; silent-but-alive is indistinguishable from deep inference at PTY layer]

tasks.title: PTY startup ANSI state reset
tasks.description: Emit ESC[0m (SGR reset) to stdout immediately after the PTY child is spawned (in cmd_shell / spawn_new_child), so a dirty terminal state from a prior session does not cause escape-code parameters to render as visible text. [CANCELLED: folded into T-121]

tasks.title: PTY stdin gating during oracle/orchestrator startup
tasks.description: Suppress forwarding of user stdin to the PTY child for a brief window (2-3 s) after agentflow shell starts and after each session restart, so incidental keystrokes (e.g. Enter pressed to dismiss a visual artifact) cannot be forwarded to Claude Code and abort the oracle/orchestrator startup sequence. [CANCELLED: folded into T-121]

tasks.title: Split commands/claude/oracle.md — size violation
tasks.description: Split commands/claude/oracle.md to resolve size violation of 160 lines (limit: 150). Violation timestamp: 2026-07-06T15:43:05.100701.

tasks.title: Wire calibrate_capacity() at handoff + add ewma_cv computation
tasks.description: Call calibrate_capacity() automatically at each handoff using ledger start/end pct (from T-163). Add ewma_cv computation to capacity_calibrator.py: track per-session pct_per_task values, compute std/mean CV across sessions, write ewma_cv + sample_count to rate_calibration_claude.json. Oracle already reads ewma_cv for task sizing — this closes the gap. Files: agentflow/shadow/capacity_calibrator.py, agentflow/shell/session_manager.py (handoff hook).

tasks.title: Oracle Phase 3 — generate artifacts in plan mode for user preview before file writes
tasks.description: Wrap oracle Phase 3 artifact generation (architecture.md, execution_plan.md, CLAUDE.md, tasks.json, design_status.md) in plan mode: show the full content as a reviewable preview before writing to disk. User approves at milestone/task-breakdown level — catches mis-sized tasks and wrong architectural decisions before they propagate to the orchestrator. Requires EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode gates around the Phase 3 Write calls in oracle.md.

tasks.title: Oracle product judgment layer — jobs-to-be-done, competitive displacement, pricing constraints, market-gated milestones
tasks.description: Extend oracle Phase 1 (market) and Phase 2 (design spar) with a product judgment layer: (1) jobs-to-be-done forcing function — what pain is acute enough to pay for today; (2) competitive displacement probe — what does the target customer already use, what is the wedge; (3) pricing/packaging as an architectural constraint — usage-based vs seat-based shapes API design, multi-tenancy, data ownership decisions; (4) milestone sequencing against market validation gates, not just technical dependencies. Update commands/claude/oracle/market.md and commands/claude/oracle/checklist.md. Add corresponding design_status.md rows for each resolved item.

tasks.title: Orchestrate startup: strip task descriptions from pending listing
tasks.description: Update startup.md Step 4 bash command to output only task_id, title, depends_on (not description) when listing pending tasks. Saves ~11K chars per startup. Full descriptions are only needed at agent-spawn time (worker reads task from tasks.json directly). One-line change to the python3 -c snippet in startup.md.

tasks.title: Orchestrate startup cache — pre-compute current_round + slim pending into .agentflow/orchestrate_cache.json
tasks.description: Write agentflow/shell/orchestrate_cache.py that generates .agentflow/orchestrate_cache.json from tasks.json + execution_plan.md + rate_calibration. Cache contains: current_round name, pending tasks for that round (id+title+deps only), all_pending count, unresolved_design_decisions count, ewma_mean_tokens, ewma_cv, sample_count. Regenerate only when source file mtimes change. Update startup.md to read cache file first (mtime check), falling back to full startup if stale. Saves 4 bash commands + 1 IDX read from orchestrator context on every startup.

tasks.title: Headroom A/B arm — headroom on/off in verbosity framework; shadow analyzer uses output_tokens delta as metric
tasks.description: Extend the verbosity A/B arm system to include a headroom on/off dimension. Proxy reads arm from .agentflow/verbosity_ab_arm.txt; for headroom-off arm, skips compress() call. Shadow analyzer reads proxy_log.jsonl output_tokens (T-{n} prerequisite) and computes mean output_tokens per arm to quantify verbosity steering + effort routing savings.

tasks.title: Design partner package — install script, README, binary bundle, settings merge
tasks.description: Create a distributable package for design partners: (1) agentflow-install.sh that runs PTY installer (T-120), merges hooks into .claude/settings.json, sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL; (2) partner README covering install, python -m agentflow shell --command claude, orchestrate loop, what to expect; (3) bundle Nuitka binary (T-112 output) + Python runtime modules into a single download artifact. Target: zero-dependency install for a new machine.

tasks.title: Headroom agent-90 spike — enable full savings profile with agentflow-safe guards
tasks.description: Activate headroom agent-90 savings profile in agentflow/proxy/server.py with three guards: (1) compress_system_messages=False to protect skill instructions; (2) extend AgentFlowHooks.compute_biases to bias=999.0 for messages containing AGENTFLOW_TASK_COMPLETE, AGENTFLOW_TASK_START, TOKENS: input=, and task ID patterns (T-\d+); (3) protect_recent=6 instead of 2 to cover a full 5-worker round. Run between orchestrate rounds only — never mid-session. Measure tokens_before/tokens_after delta vs current baseline in proxy_log.jsonl.

tasks.title: Split agentflow/shell/session_manager.py — size violation
tasks.description: Split agentflow/shell/session_manager.py to resolve size violation of 252 lines (limit: 250). Violation timestamp: 2026-07-04T17:44:33.317580.

tasks.title: Hook audit log + PTY threshold state snapshot — diagnose handoff stalls
tasks.description: PostToolUse hook appends to .agentflow/hook_audit.jsonl (task_id, action, tasks_json_write ok|failed, timestamp). output_handler.py _log_audit snapshots _task_start_tokens at every threshold evaluation. Enables replay of exactly what was in-memory when handoff gate checked.

tasks.title: PR URL registry — write task_id→PR URL on gh pr create; use gh pr view for merge detection
tasks.description:
Fix unreliable PR merge detection in post_tool_use_agent.py by replacing fuzzy title matching with deterministic gh pr view state checks.

1. On Bash PostToolUse: detect gh pr create in command; extract PR URL from output (last non-empty line); derive task_id from branch name (pattern task/T-XXX-*) or from tasks_in_flight.json single entry; write/merge {task_id: pr_url} into .agentflow/task_prs.json.

2. On merge detection: load task_prs.json; for each in-flight task with a known PR URL run gh pr view <url> --json state (timeout 5s); treat state==MERGED as confirmed. Fall back to existing title-match heuristic only for tasks with no registered URL.

tests/hooks/test_post_tool_use_agent_pr_registry.py: test URL write on gh pr create output; test gh pr view detection returns MERGED; test fallback path when task_prs.json absent.
